title: This Is The First Real Shape Of AGI: Fusion Agents
summary: The video argues that multi-agent systems capable of building applications, coordinating work, and deploying services represent the first tangible architecture of AGI. It contrasts reasoning-layer advances with a new "working layer" where AI agents can execute complex tasks end-to-end.
writeup: The video argues that AI agents able to build interactive tools, coordinate across multiple workers, and deploy executable services represent an early shape of AGI, framing this as a shift from conversational AI toward real-world execution. It contrasts this transition with prior reasoning advances, positioning multi-agent coordination and deployment as the missing layer. The central takeaway: orchestration and deployment, not reasoning alone, are presented as what moves agents from talk to action.
